stem: A warehouse loading dock is 12 feet above the ground. A ramp is built from the ground to the dock at a 37-degree angle with the horizontal. Given that sin 37° ≈ 3/5, cos 37° ≈ 4/5, and tan 37° ≈ 3/4, what is the length of the ramp, in feet?
explanation: In this right triangle, the dock height (12 feet) is the side opposite the 37° angle and the ramp is the hypotenuse. Using sin 37° = opposite/hypotenuse: 3/5 = 12/hypotenuse, so hypotenuse = 12 ÷ (3/5) = 12 × (5/3) = 60/3 = 20 feet.

distractorRationale.A: Used cos instead of sin in the denominator — computed height/cos 37° instead of height/sin 37°: 12 ÷ (4/5) = 12 × (5/4) = 15
distractorRationale.B: Used tan instead of sin in the denominator — computed height/tan 37° instead of height/sin 37°: 12 ÷ (3/4) = 12 × (4/3) = 16
distractorRationale.D: Used wrong special triangle ratio — applied the 30-60-90 rule (hypotenuse = 2 × opposite) instead of using the given trig value: 2 × 12 = 24
